[Gf. mTHcf—
♦T II
^ CRT II
Here there is a ticklish problem : In almost all the cases the
depth of water is given in Purusas and the distance in cubits. How-
ever, Bhajjotpala interprets in this verse :
When water is said to be just underneath a thing, there is no
necessity for mentioning any distance from it. However, the state-

Exploration of Water Springs LIV 619
ment of Sage Sarasvata, which is in the Ablative case
gives room for doubt whether the reference is to horizontal length
or not. In our previous edition (page 477) we have taken it to mean
the horizontal distance of 4 cubits. If this be the correct interpreta-
tion, we might take the depth from the previous verse, viz.
i.e 17} cubits.]
